modules.name: Dashboard (module-dashboard, owner-dashboard)
modules.summary: No form submission flows detected in sampled files. The module is read-only dashboard and embed management; user input is limited to search and bookmark naming, which use Luna Input inline without a SmartForm/SchemaForm wrapper.
recommendations: If bookmark creation grows into a multi-field form, evaluate adopting SchemaForm for validation and consistency

modules.name: Controls (module-assessments, manage-hub, module-resource-planner)
modules.summary: Forms are present in entity-actions and manage-control-environment modals. Luna Form and Field are used in manage-hub modals, but module-assessments has no SchemaForm adoption despite complex assessment response flows. Submit-for-review uses a task-based pattern without a form wrapper, making validation ad-hoc.
risks: Assessment response workflows rely on ad-hoc validation without a SchemaForm engine
manage-hub modals use Luna Form but lack FormError components for field-level error display
module-resource-planner has no form wrapper; booking inputs are unmanaged
recommendations: Adopt SchemaForm for the assessment response submission flow to centralize validation
Add FormError to manage-hub modal forms to surface server-side validation errors inline
Wrap resource booking input fields in a Luna Form component for consistent state management

modules.name: Risks (module-risks)
modules.summary: Luna Form and Field are used in manage-key-risk-indicator.gts and manage-risk-appetite.gts. FormError is imported and used. The explanation-response-form-input.gts uses a custom tracked-object pattern for form state rather than a Luna Form wrapper, which is a consistency gap.
risks: Explanation response form uses a custom tracked-state pattern instead of Luna Form for validation
Risk appetite bracket editing uses custom range/table editor components without a unified form engine
No SchemaForm adoption despite the module having template-driven form content
recommendations: Migrate explanation-response-form-input.gts to use Luna Form for consistent validation and error handling
Evaluate SchemaForm for the KRI creation modal where field definitions are already dynamic

modules.name: CrossComply (module-compliance-assessments)
modules.summary: Assessment creation wizard uses Luna Select and custom option patterns without a Luna Form wrapper. Wizard step navigation is custom-built. No SchemaForm adoption found. Some steps use inline formatMessage directly in template with no form state tracking.
risks: Multi-step wizard has no unified form state or validation engine; each step manages state independently
Evidence request configuration step has no error handling for invalid selections
No SchemaForm adoption despite complex data-entry wizard flows
recommendations: Evaluate SchemaForm or a wizard-scoped Luna Form for multi-step assessment creation
Add FormError or inline error display to each wizard step that has required fields
Document the wizard form state architecture so new steps follow the established pattern

modules.name: Issues (module-issues)
modules.summary: view-issue-panel.gjs uses SchemaForm with a v1SubmitHandlers pattern and schemaFormEngineResource, which is good adoption. However the file is a large legacy Ember classic component, and the SchemaForm coexists with inline form fields and InlineComponentForm, creating mixed patterns.
risks: SchemaForm and InlineComponentForm coexist in view-issue-panel.gjs, creating inconsistent UX for field editing
Legacy classic component architecture makes it difficult to add validation to non-SchemaForm fields
Action plan status changes use direct task calls without form-level validation
recommendations: Migrate inline form fields in view-issue-panel.gjs to use the existing SchemaForm engine where possible
Remove InlineComponentForm in favor of SchemaForm as the single field-editing mechanism
Add a validation check before action plan status transitions using the schema-form error model

modules.name: OpsAudit (module-opsaudits)
modules.summary: Settings forms use ModelProjectOptionToggle for checkbox-style options, which is a consistent pattern within the module. However there is no Luna Form wrapper around the settings form group, no FormError for save failures, and no SchemaForm adoption for assessment template configuration.
risks: Settings form group lacks a Luna Form wrapper; save errors surface only via notification toasts
No SchemaForm adoption for audit template settings despite dynamic field patterns
recommendations: Wrap the audit settings form group in a Luna Form component to centralize save state and error handling
Consider SchemaForm for audit template configuration fields that vary by template type

modules.name: WorkStream (module-tasks)
modules.summary: task-details-tab-form.gjs uses Luna Form with @autosave={{true}} and onSubmit handler, which is a strong pattern. changedAttributes-based delta saves are well-structured. FormInputError is used for title validation. The main gap is that task-user-selects.gts uses direct savePayload calls outside any form wrapper.
risks: task-user-selects.gts bypasses form state management with direct savePayload calls; errors surface only via notification
Untyped .gjs file for task-details-tab-form makes TypeScript-level form validation impossible
recommendations: Wrap user-select save flows in a form context or add explicit error display for each save call
Migrate task-details-tab-form.gjs to .gts to enable TypeScript validation of form field types

modules.name: BCM (module-bcm)
modules.summary: add-bcm-function.gts uses SchemaFormEngine with a proper formTemplate lookup and SchemaForm render, which is exemplary adoption. create-category.gts uses Luna Form, Field, Input, FormError correctly. The module is small but well-structured for its form flows.
risks: SchemaForm relies on preloaded form-templates via ux.template; failure to preload silently breaks the form
RelatedProcess selection uses a custom action handler outside the SchemaForm engine
recommendations: Add a graceful fallback or loading state if the form-template is not preloaded in add-bcm-function.gts
Integrate relatedProcess selection into the SchemaForm field schema if supported

modules.name: Settings (module-admin, site-configuration)
modules.summary: Settings uses SmartForm template management (smart-form-templates-list.gts) and SchemaForm template creation (create-smart-form-template-modal.gts), demonstrating awareness of the SmartForms system. However most admin settings forms use Luna Field/Input directly without FormError or a SchemaForm engine.
risks: Most admin settings forms lack FormError components; validation errors surface only via notification toasts
tag-list and requesting-party-list create/edit flows have no form state management
recommendations: Add FormError to admin settings forms to display field-level validation errors inline
Adopt SchemaForm for the requesting-party creation flow as a reference implementation

modules.name: ESG (module-esg)
modules.summary: No form components detected in the 4 sampled ESG files. The module is primarily a read-only topics list view. ESG metric value form (details-form.gts) lives in module-dashboard and is evaluated there.
recommendations: As ESG topic creation and editing grows, adopt SchemaForm for consistency with BCM and Exceptions

modules.name: TPRM (module-tprm)
modules.summary: advanced-notifications-trigger-form.gjs uses Luna Form, Input, and Select but is an untyped .gjs file with no FormError or field-level validation. Score creation uses custom task-based save patterns. No SchemaForm adoption despite the module having multi-step questionnaire configuration flows.
risks: advanced-notifications-trigger-form.gjs has no field-level validation or FormError display
Notification trigger creation fails silently if the recurring-schedule API call errors mid-task
Large module (114 files) with no SchemaForm adoption for questionnaire template management
recommendations: Add FormError to advanced-notifications-trigger-form.gjs and handle API errors with field-level display
Evaluate SchemaForm for questionnaire template configuration where field definitions are dynamic
Migrate advanced-notifications-trigger-form.gjs to .gts for TypeScript safety

modules.name: Narratives (module-narratives)
modules.summary: Only 1 component file exists (narrative-list.gjs), which is a list view with no form submission flow. Narrative creation is routed to a WorkStream project creation modal that lives outside this module.
recommendations: When narrative editing is built out within this module, adopt SchemaForm for field definitions

modules.name: RegComply (module-regulations, libraries/module-regulations)
modules.summary: Library modals (add-regulation.gts, map-policies.gts, restore-regulation.gts, delete-regulation.gts) use Luna Form and Field patterns. The module-regulations app-side tables use SchemaTable, not SchemaForm. SmartForm adoption is partial; modal creation flows are well-structured but lack FormError in some modals.
risks: Some library modals may lack FormError for server-side validation display
SchemaTable-driven regulatory signal views have no inline editing form
recommendations: Audit each library modal in libraries/module-regulations/package/src/components/modals/ for FormError presence
Add inline edit support via SchemaForm for regulatory signal detail fields if editing is planned

modules.name: Exceptions (module-exceptions)
modules.summary: exception-request-form.gts wraps SchemaForm with a SchemaFormEngine, with a clear form ID, onSubmit handler, and graceful 'No form template found' fallback. exception-request-new.gts and the modal variant both use this shared form component. This is among the strongest SchemaForm adoption patterns in the codebase.
risks: SchemaForm rendering depends on a non-null engine prop; null engine displays a plain text fallback rather than a loading state
recommendations: Replace the plain text 'No form template found' fallback with a loading skeleton or error state component

modules.name: Integrations (module-integrations)
modules.summary: automation-credential.gts uses Luna Form, Field, Input, and Select with proper hash-based form block patterns. AnimatedEach is used for connector list rendering. No SchemaForm adoption found despite credential configuration being a multi-field form. FormError is not imported in sampled files.
risks: Credential creation form lacks FormError; validation errors are not displayed inline
Connector version selection uses direct action handlers without form validation
recommendations: Add FormError to automation-credential.gts to display server validation errors at the field level
Evaluate SchemaForm for credential parameter configuration where connector schemas vary by type

modules.name: Automations (module-automations)
modules.summary: automation-details-fields.gts uses Luna Form-like patterns with Field, Input, TextArea and action-based onChange handlers. monitor-details-form.gts uses a similar pattern. No SchemaForm adoption found despite the module having automation workflow parameter schemas that would benefit from schema-driven forms.
risks: Automation details form uses onChange callbacks without centralized form validation or error display
Automation parameter fields are rendered via ParameterField component with no unified form engine
recommendations: Adopt SchemaForm for automation parameter configuration using the automation workflow's paramsSchema
Add FormError to automation-details-fields.gts for inline validation feedback

modules.name: Inventory (module-inventory)
modules.summary: Inventory module has 1 file using SchemaForm. Breadcrumb and hierarchy navigation are read-only. Platform hierarchy editing likely uses inline forms. Luna Field and Input patterns are present but FormError adoption is unverified across the module's form components.
risks: Partial SchemaForm adoption; 1 of 98 files uses SchemaForm, suggesting most edit flows use ad-hoc patterns
Organizational hierarchy updates use direct savePayload calls without form-level validation
recommendations: Expand SchemaForm adoption to inventory entity creation and editing flows
Add field-level validation and FormError to the primary entity edit form

modules.name: AI Governance (module-ai-governance)
modules.summary: Only 1 component file exists and it is a pass-through container with no form elements. The module is in early stages.
recommendations: Adopt SchemaForm from the start when AI Governance entity creation and editing is built out

modules.name: Files (files)
modules.summary: google-file-upload.gjs uses a native @ember/component Input with document.getElementById, bypassing Luna Form entirely. No FormError, no form state management, no validation. File operations rely on Promise-based error handling surfaced only via notification toasts.
risks: Native browser Input and Ember classic Input bypass all Luna Form validation and error display patterns
File upload errors are caught in a try/catch and shown as notification toasts only; no inline field error display
document.getElementById usage creates tight coupling to DOM structure and breaks encapsulation
recommendations: Replace document.getElementById file input with a Luna or Glimmer-native file input component
Add inline error display within the file upload component rather than relying solely on notification toasts
Evaluate whether file-dropzone.gts (used in other modules) can replace google-file-upload.gjs custom implementation

modules.name: Timesheets (module-timesheets)
modules.summary: Only 1 component file exists (list.gts). The module is a read-only list view with date range filtering via SelectDateRange. No form submission flow detected.
recommendations: If timesheet entry creation or editing is added, adopt Luna Form and SchemaForm for consistency

modules.name: Automated Security Questionnaires (module-questionnaires)
modules.summary: create-questionnaire-template.gts and edit-brackets.gjs use Luna Form, Field, and Checkbox patterns. automated-questionnaire-response.gts uses TrackedObject for generation options state. edit-bundle-reviewers.gts uses a form-like pattern. SchemaForm is not used in questionnaire forms but Luna Form adoption is strong.
risks: automated-questionnaire-response.gts uses TrackedObject for form state rather than Luna Form; no validation or FormError
legacy-edit-brackets.gts implies a parallel legacy implementation that should be retired
recommendations: Migrate automated-questionnaire-response.gts form state to a Luna Form wrapper for validation consistency
Remove or migrate legacy-edit-brackets.gts to consolidate on edit-brackets.gjs as the single implementation

modules.name: ITRM / Cyber Risk (module-itrm)
modules.summary: exception-request-modal.gts and questionnaire-answer-modal.gts both use SchemaForm, demonstrating strong SmartForms adoption. questionnaire-send-modal.gts also uses SchemaForm. The module has consistent SchemaForm usage across its 3 form-bearing components.
risks: SchemaForm modals depend on preloaded form templates; no graceful degradation if template fetch fails
recommendations: Add a loading skeleton or error fallback in each SchemaForm modal for cases where the form template is unavailable

modules.name: Other (shared, application-chrome)
modules.summary: shared/select-review-type-and-reviewers.gts is a complex form-like component for reviewer assignment. application-chrome has navigation forms. Luna Form adoption in shared is inconsistent; some components use it, others manage state manually. No SchemaForm usage detected.
risks: select-review-type-and-reviewers.gts manages complex form state without a Luna Form wrapper
Shared form components are used across many modules; inconsistency here propagates across the codebase
recommendations: Refactor select-review-type-and-reviewers.gts to use a Luna Form wrapper for consistent state and validation
Establish a shared form component library pattern for reviewer and user-selection forms used across modules
